.\docs\polaris_fundamental_rearchitecture_plan.md:24:- **A. Scholarly-graph authority (global, language-agnostic).** OpenAlex/Crossref give `cited_by_count`, venue `summary_stats.h_index` + `2yr_mean_citedness`, `is_core` (vetted-venue), `is_in_doaj` vs missing-DOAJ + high `apc_prices` (predatory-OA smell), `is_retracted` (hard exclude). (Codex correction 2026-05-31: there is NO `is_peer_reviewed` boolean on OpenAlex; peer-review status is INFERRED from `is_core` + venue `type` + Crossref peer-review metadata, not read from a flag.) Citation/link-graph authority scored PageRank-style — a source authoritative sources cite — generalizes to any field. (Brin-Page; Ding co-citation authority https://arxiv.org/abs/1012.4872 ; fields confirmed on developers.openalex.org.) Replaces `PEER_REVIEWED_*`/`LOW_QUALITY_OA_DOMAINS` frozensets with a *measured* venue score identical for NEJM, a Japanese physics journal, or a Brazilian agronomy journal.
.\docs\polaris_fundamental_rearchitecture_plan.md:25:- **B. Institutional / primary-vs-secondary (the region generalization).** PRIMARY signal = OpenAlex Institution ROR `type` ∈ {Government, Education, Healthcare…} + `country_code` (this is what actually resolves `bundesbank.de`, `rbi.org.in`, `canada.ca` — which are NOT under a gov-suffix). SECONDARY assist = the public-suffix-list gov-suffix patterns (`*.gov`, `*.gc.ca`, `*.go.jp`, `*.gouv.fr`, `*.go.ke`) for hosts that DO carry one. (Codex correction 2026-05-31: PSL is a DNS suffix list and explicitly NOT a trust/authority signal; it covers `*.gov`-style hosts only and MISSES `canada.ca`/`bundesbank.de`/`rbi.org.in`, so PSL alone is insufficient — ROR institution-type is the load-bearing official-source signal, PSL is a cheap pre-filter, and an issuer-self-description check backstops both.) Replaces `REGULATORY_DOMAINS` + `GOV_AGENCY_DOMAINS`.
.\docs\polaris_fundamental_rearchitecture_plan.md:27:- **D. Corroboration — the sovereign trust multiplier and load-bearing signal.** Count of *independent* hosts/institutions carrying the same finding after dedup-by-finding (#7). This is **Knowledge-Based Trust** (Google, arXiv 1502.03519 https://arxiv.org/pdf/1502.03519): trust a source for the correctness of facts the rest of the corpus independently confirms, not link popularity. It is intrinsically domain-general (asks only "do independent sources agree?") and is the **primary sovereign defense** for fields with thin OpenAlex coverage, because we compute it ourselves from the corpus with no external authority service. Also feeds #16 conflict surfacing.
.\docs\polaris_fundamental_rearchitecture_plan.md:28:- **E. Recency/temporal-fit.** OpenAlex year / `Last-Modified` vs the planner's declared recency need.
.\docs\polaris_fundamental_rearchitecture_plan.md:36:**The over-fit gate — a CALIBRATED AUTHORITY CONTRACT, not a grep (Codex correction 2026-05-31, iter 1 OVERCLAIMS → adopted):** the zero-host grep is NECESSARY but NOT SUFFICIENT — it misses platform-host literals (`medium.com/@`, `linkedin.com/pulse`), suffix literals, keyword routers, and any source-policy fixture. The real acceptance contract is FOUR parts: (1) **no literal hosts/suffixes/platform-strings in code** — PSL, ROR-type maps, the OpenAlex surface, and the structural/junk patterns are all loaded as **VERSIONED EXTERNAL DATA** (config/data files, audited + diffable), never inlined; (2) the zero-host grep over the *code* (not the data) returns zero; (3) **explicit coverage/uncertainty scoring** — every source gets an `authority_confidence` alongside its score, so a thin-OpenAlex / grey-lit source is flagged LOW-CONFIDENCE rather than silently mis-labeled authoritative-or-junk; (4) **adversarial thin-field fixtures** — a test set of grey-lit / non-English / niche-regional sources (African energy grey-lit, regional policy) that the model must score with honest low-confidence, not false-authority. New region → ROR + PSL data; new field → `is_core`/`summary_stats`; new language → Serper locale + DOI graph; new-field junk → fails `is_in_doaj`/`is_core` + trips structural detector + zero corroboration → rejected without ever being named. `tier_classifier.py` fails part (1) with hundreds of named hosts; the replacement must pass all four.
.\docs\polaris_fundamental_rearchitecture_plan.md:48:| **Source discovery by need-type** — open-web (Serper) + generic academic graph (OpenAlex/Semantic Scholar) fan-out; adapter registry keyed by *declared evidence-need* (primary-lit/legal/regulatory/statistical/code), NOT a domain enum; PSL gov-suffix discovery channel narrowed to extracted jurisdiction | `domain_backends.py:443-455` 4-branch `if domain ==` switch + `_POLICY_SITE_FILTERS:150-161` (US-only) | DR survey arXiv 2508.12752 (https://arxiv.org/html/2508.12752v1) | `discovery/source_adapter_registry.py` + `need_type_router.py`; refactor `domain_backends.py` |
.\docs\polaris_fundamental_rearchitecture_plan.md:112:What each phase must measure before any spend (all offline/shadow): Ph 0a — T1-T7 reproduction ≥95% + the full 4-part calibrated authority contract (§2: data-not-code + zero-host grep over code + per-source authority_confidence + adversarial thin-field fixtures) + cross-field scoring; Ph 0b — byte-identical factual mode + regression suite green; Ph 1-6 — the per-phase offline exits in the table (no fetch / fixture-only where possible). Authority-model authority-API calls (OpenAlex/Crossref are free; Serper is the metered search backend), the Sentinel intrinsic-scorer at corpus scale (batch it), and the Judge reasoning-entailment per synthesis sentence are the cost lines to watch — measure token/call counts in shadow before Ph 8. **Only Phase 8 spends** on a paid sweep, and only after 0-7 pass offline; this prevents billing the generator into the clinical trap.

.\docs\polaris_fundamental_rearchitecture_plan.md:135:**The single biggest risk:** for non-scholarly open-web sources in thin-OpenAlex fields (African energy grey-lit, niche regional policy), the sovereign web-authority signal is weak — there is no sovereign backlink graph and US domain-authority vendors are off the table for #23. The **corroboration signal (independent-host agreement computed from the corpus itself)** is the primary sovereign defense and is the part most worth Codex's scrutiny; if it under-performs, thin-field credibility degrades and the global-source promise is only as strong as corroboration depth allows. Adjacent second risk: gap 9 is the dimension most likely to still lose if under-built — the substrate exists but has never been wired to the sweep, given a provenance contract, or run blinded against a frontier sensitivity table.

.\docs\polaris_fundamental_rearchitecture_plan.md:24:- **A. Scholarly-graph authority (global, language-agnostic).** OpenAlex/Crossref give `cited_by_count`, venue `summary_stats.h_index` + `2yr_mean_citedness`, `is_core` (vetted-venue), `is_in_doaj` vs missing-DOAJ + high `apc_prices` (predatory-OA smell), `is_retracted` (hard exclude). (Codex correction 2026-05-31: there is NO `is_peer_reviewed` boolean on OpenAlex; peer-review status is INFERRED from `is_core` + venue `type` + Crossref peer-review metadata, not read from a flag.) Citation/link-graph authority scored PageRank-style — a source authoritative sources cite — generalizes to any field. (Brin-Page; Ding co-citation authority https://arxiv.org/abs/1012.4872 ; fields confirmed on developers.openalex.org.) Replaces `PEER_REVIEWED_*`/`LOW_QUALITY_OA_DOMAINS` frozensets with a *measured* venue score identical for NEJM, a Japanese physics journal, or a Brazilian agronomy journal.
.\docs\polaris_fundamental_rearchitecture_plan.md:25:- **B. Institutional / primary-vs-secondary (the region generalization).** PRIMARY signal = OpenAlex Institution ROR `type` ∈ {Government, Education, Healthcare…} + `country_code` (this is what actually resolves `bundesbank.de`, `rbi.org.in`, `canada.ca` — which are NOT under a gov-suffix). SECONDARY assist = the public-suffix-list gov-suffix patterns (`*.gov`, `*.gc.ca`, `*.go.jp`, `*.gouv.fr`, `*.go.ke`) for hosts that DO carry one. (Codex correction 2026-05-31: PSL is a DNS suffix list and explicitly NOT a trust/authority signal; it covers `*.gov`-style hosts only and MISSES `canada.ca`/`bundesbank.de`/`rbi.org.in`, so PSL alone is insufficient — ROR institution-type is the load-bearing official-source signal, PSL is a cheap pre-filter, and an issuer-self-description check backstops both.) Replaces `REGULATORY_DOMAINS` + `GOV_AGENCY_DOMAINS`.
.\docs\polaris_fundamental_rearchitecture_plan.md:27:- **D. Corroboration — the sovereign trust multiplier and load-bearing signal.** Count of *independent* hosts/institutions carrying the same finding after dedup-by-finding (#7). This is **Knowledge-Based Trust** (Google, arXiv 1502.03519 https://arxiv.org/pdf/1502.03519): trust a source for the correctness of facts the rest of the corpus independently confirms, not link popularity. It is intrinsically domain-general (asks only "do independent sources agree?") and is the **primary sovereign defense** for fields with thin OpenAlex coverage, because we compute it ourselves from the corpus with no external authority service. Also feeds #16 conflict surfacing.
.\docs\polaris_fundamental_rearchitecture_plan.md:28:- **E. Recency/temporal-fit.** OpenAlex year / `Last-Modified` vs the planner's declared recency need.
.\docs\polaris_fundamental_rearchitecture_plan.md:36:**The over-fit gate — a CALIBRATED AUTHORITY CONTRACT, not a grep (Codex correction 2026-05-31, iter 1 OVERCLAIMS → adopted):** the zero-host grep is NECESSARY but NOT SUFFICIENT — it misses platform-host literals (`medium.com/@`, `linkedin.com/pulse`), suffix literals, keyword routers, and any source-policy fixture. The real acceptance contract is FOUR parts: (1) **no literal hosts/suffixes/platform-strings in code** — PSL, ROR-type maps, the OpenAlex surface, and the structural/junk patterns are all loaded as **VERSIONED EXTERNAL DATA** (config/data files, audited + diffable), never inlined; (2) the zero-host grep over the *code* (not the data) returns zero; (3) **explicit coverage/uncertainty scoring** — every source gets an `authority_confidence` alongside its score, so a thin-OpenAlex / grey-lit source is flagged LOW-CONFIDENCE rather than silently mis-labeled authoritative-or-junk; (4) **adversarial thin-field fixtures** — a test set of grey-lit / non-English / niche-regional sources (African energy grey-lit, regional policy) that the model must score with honest low-confidence, not false-authority. New region → ROR + PSL data; new field → `is_core`/`summary_stats`; new language → Serper locale + DOI graph; new-field junk → fails `is_in_doaj`/`is_core` + trips structural detector + zero corroboration → rejected without ever being named. `tier_classifier.py` fails part (1) with hundreds of named hosts; the replacement must pass all four.
.\docs\polaris_fundamental_rearchitecture_plan.md:48:| **Source discovery by need-type** — open-web (Serper) + generic academic graph (OpenAlex/Semantic Scholar) fan-out; adapter registry keyed by *declared evidence-need* (primary-lit/legal/regulatory/statistical/code), NOT a domain enum; PSL gov-suffix discovery channel narrowed to extracted jurisdiction | `domain_backends.py:443-455` 4-branch `if domain ==` switch + `_POLICY_SITE_FILTERS:150-161` (US-only) | DR survey arXiv 2508.12752 (https://arxiv.org/html/2508.12752v1) | `discovery/source_adapter_registry.py` + `need_type_router.py`; refactor `domain_backends.py` |
.\docs\polaris_fundamental_rearchitecture_plan.md:112:What each phase must measure before any spend (all offline/shadow): Ph 0a — T1-T7 reproduction ≥95% + the full 4-part calibrated authority contract (§2: data-not-code + zero-host grep over code + per-source authority_confidence + adversarial thin-field fixtures) + cross-field scoring; Ph 0b — byte-identical factual mode + regression suite green; Ph 1-6 — the per-phase offline exits in the table (no fetch / fixture-only where possible). Authority-model authority-API calls (OpenAlex/Crossref are free; Serper is the metered search backend), the Sentinel intrinsic-scorer at corpus scale (batch it), and the Judge reasoning-entailment per synthesis sentence are the cost lines to watch — measure token/call counts in shadow before Ph 8. **Only Phase 8 spends** on a paid sweep, and only after 0-7 pass offline; this prevents billing the generator into the clinical trap.

.\docs\polaris_fundamental_rearchitecture_plan.md:135:**The single biggest risk:** for non-scholarly open-web sources in thin-OpenAlex fields (African energy grey-lit, niche regional policy), the sovereign web-authority signal is weak — there is no sovereign backlink graph and US domain-authority vendors are off the table for #23. .\outputs\codex_findings\full_scale_pass_9\findings.md:24:Article-type arm: sound. `_title_is_diagnostic_for_article_type` only checks explicit title labels, so a title such as `Clinical practice guideline for X` or `Tirzepatide efficacy: a systematic review and meta-analysis` makes R8b advisory and lets R9/R10 classify from title/OpenAlex. This prevents body false positives from changing one article type into another.
.\outputs\codex_findings\full_scale_pass_9\findings.md:26:Primary-study arm: mostly sound. It requires both `_detect_primary_study_signal(title)` and OpenAlex journal/peer-reviewed evidence with `publication_type in ("article", "review")`. This is the right architectural bound: a random web page or repository item titled like a trial still lets body override fire.
.\outputs\codex_findings\full_scale_pass_9\findings.md:57:One residual over-protection risk remains. The primary-study marker `effect of` / `effects of` is broad. In the scenario `Effect of tirzepatide on beta-cell function` with a legitimate `CASE_REPORT` body signal and OpenAlex journal article metadata, R8b suppresses the body demotion and R9 returns `T1`. Most legitimate case reports, systematic reviews, and guidelines should carry title labels caught by the article-type arm or R9 title demoters, so this is non-blocking but should be monitored.
.\outputs\codex_findings\full_scale_pass_9\findings.md:61:One residual under-protection risk remains by design: primary journal papers with bare titles and no positive primary marker are not protected from false body signals. Example: a true primary paper titled only `Tirzepatide in type 2 diabetes` would not satisfy the primary-study arm; if the detector emits `GUIDELINE`, R8b still demotes to `T4`. This is the cost of keeping the gate conservative and avoiding over-trusting OpenAlex article+journal alone.

docs\polaris_fundamental_rearchitecture_plan.md:36:**The over-fit gate — a CALIBRATED AUTHORITY CONTRACT, not a grep (Codex correction 2026-05-31, iter 1 OVERCLAIMS → adopted):** the zero-host grep is NECESSARY but NOT SUFFICIENT — it misses platform-host literals (`medium.com/@`, `linkedin.com/pulse`), suffix literals, keyword routers, and any source-policy fixture. The real acceptance contract is FOUR parts: (1) **no literal hosts/suffixes/platform-strings in code** — PSL, ROR-type maps, the OpenAlex surface, and the structural/junk patterns are all loaded as **VERSIONED EXTERNAL DATA** (config/data files, audited + diffable), never inlined; (2) the zero-host grep over the *code* (not the data) returns zero; (3) **explicit coverage/uncertainty scoring** — every source gets an `authority_confidence` alongside its score, so a thin-OpenAlex / grey-lit source is flagged LOW-CONFIDENCE rather than silently mis-labeled authoritative-or-junk; (4) **adversarial thin-field fixtures** — a test set of grey-lit / non-English / niche-regional sources (African energy grey-lit, regional policy) that the model must score with honest low-confidence, not false-authority. New region → ROR + PSL data; new field → `is_core`/`summary_stats`; new language → Serper locale + DOI graph; new-field junk → fails `is_in_doaj`/`is_core` + trips structural detector + zero corroboration → rejected without ever being named. `tier_classifier.py` fails part (1) with hundreds of named hosts; the replacement must pass all four.

.codex\I-meta-005-phase-0a\codex_brief_verdict.txt:3229:.\docs\polaris_fundamental_rearchitecture_plan.md:36:**The over-fit gate — a CALIBRATED AUTHORITY CONTRACT, not a grep (Codex correction 2026-05-31, iter 1 OVERCLAIMS → adopted):** the zero-host grep is NECESSARY but NOT SUFFICIENT — it misses platform-host literals (`medium.com/@`, `linkedin.com/pulse`), suffix literals, keyword routers, and any source-policy fixture. The real acceptance contract is FOUR parts: (1) **no literal hosts/suffixes/platform-strings in code** — PSL, ROR-type maps, the OpenAlex surface, and the structural/junk patterns are all loaded as **VERSIONED EXTERNAL DATA** (config/data files, audited + diffable), never inlined; (2) the zero-host grep over the *code* (not the data) returns zero; (3) **explicit coverage/uncertainty scoring** — every source gets an `authority_confidence` alongside its score, so a thin-OpenAlex / grey-lit source is flagged LOW-CONFIDENCE rather than silently mis-labeled authoritative-or-junk; (4) **adversarial thin-field fixtures** — a test set of grey-lit / non-English / niche-regional sources (African energy grey-lit, regional policy) that the model must score with honest low-confidence, not false-authority. New region → ROR + PSL data; new field → `is_core`/`summary_stats`; new language → Serper locale + DOI graph; new-field junk → fails `is_in_doaj`/`is_core` + trips structural detector + zero corroboration → rejected without ever being named. `tier_classifier.py` fails part (1) with hundreds of named hosts; the replacement must pass all four.
.codex\I-meta-005-phase-0a\codex_brief_verdict.txt:3231:.\docs\polaris_fundamental_rearchitecture_plan.md:112:What each phase must measure before any spend (all offline/shadow): Ph 0a — T1-T7 reproduction ≥95% + the full 4-part calibrated authority contract (§2: data-not-code + zero-host grep over code + per-source authority_confidence + adversarial thin-field fixtures) + cross-field scoring; Ph 0b — byte-identical factual mode + regression suite green; Ph 1-6 — the per-phase offline exits in the table (no fetch / fixture-only where possible). Authority-model authority-API calls (OpenAlex/Crossref are free; Serper is the metered search backend), the Sentinel intrinsic-scorer at corpus scale (batch it), and the Judge reasoning-entailment per synthesis sentence are the cost lines to watch — measure token/call counts in shadow before Ph 8. **Only Phase 8 spends** on a paid sweep, and only after 0-7 pass offline; this prevents billing the generator into the clinical trap.

 283: ### C1 (P1) — the AuthoritySignals input bridge (load-bearing fields must REACH the classifier)
 284: Extending `OpenAlexWork` alone does NOT deliver fields to `_classify_via_authority_model`. `ClassificationSignals`
 285: (`tier_classifier.py:83`) carries only legacy OpenAlex hints, and `live_retriever.py:1789` populates only those.
 286: FIX: add an ADDITIVE `AuthoritySignals` payload (new optional dataclass, default None — backward-compatible) carrying
 287: `cited_by_count, source_id, venue_summary_stats, is_core, is_in_doaj, apc_prices, ror_id, institution_type,
 288: country_code, publication_year`. Wire it end-to-end: `openalex_client._parse_work` populates it → `OpenAlexWork`
 289: (+ `authority_tier_t7`) carries it → `live_retriever.py:1789-1811` passes it into `ClassificationSignals` (additive
 290: field) → `score_source_authority(signals)` reads it. When the payload is absent/partial → `authority_confidence=LOW`
 291: (fail-honest, never fabricate authority). The frozen S2 fixtures MUST capture this payload (see C2 fixture note).
 292: 
 293: ### C2 (P1) — correct OpenAlex retrieval contract (root /works select + /sources lookup + real cache migration)
 294: OpenAlex `select=` is ROOT-LEVEL ONLY (rejects nested props). Therefore:
 295: - `/works` `select=`: `id,doi,title,type,publication_year,cited_by_count,is_retracted,primary_location,authorships`
 296:   (root fields only; `primary_location.source.id` is reachable as part of the `primary_location` object).
 297: - `summary_stats` (h_index / 2yr_mean_citedness), `apc_prices`, `is_core`, `is_in_doaj` are **SOURCE** fields →
 298:   a SEPARATE `/sources/{source_id}` fetch keyed by `primary_location.source.id`, with its own cache table. Absent →
 299:   `authority_confidence=LOW`.
 300: - SQLite cache: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` does NOT add columns to an existing cache → ship a real VERSIONED
 301:   MIGRATION (bump cache schema version + `ALTER TABLE`/rebuild path), do not silently no-op on an old cache.
 302: 
 303: ### C3 (P2) — complete the consumer map
 304: `src/polaris_graph/honest_pipeline.py:60,167` is also a direct `classify_source_tier` caller; it reads only
 305: `.tier.value/.confidence/.matched_rules/.reasons` (legacy) → still wedge-safe, but it is added to the consumer map
 306: so "all consumers checked" is honest.
 307: 
 308: ### C4 (P2) — institution-type vocabulary
 309: OpenAlex institution `type` includes `archive`; native ROR `types` also includes `funder`. The versioned
 310: institution-type→class data map MUST handle `archive` and `funder` explicitly (deterministic class or intentional
 311: LOW-confidence), not leave them to fall through.
 312:

 573: def _openalex_enrich(url: str, title: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
 574:     """Query OpenAlex for pub_type / source_type / is_peer_reviewed.
 575: 
 576:     M-13 (BUG-M-13, Codex pass 13): prefer DOI-based lookup over
 577:     title search. When the URL embeds a DOI (Frontiers, JAMA, NEJM,
 578:     OUP, etc.), OpenAlex's /works/doi:<doi> endpoint is exact and
 579:     always returns the full display_name. Title-based search often
 580:     fails when Serper truncated the title or returned a variant
 581:     that OpenAlex doesn't index. Falls back to title search when no
 582:     DOI can be extracted (e.g., MDPI URLs, publisher blog posts).
 583:     """
 584:     try:
 585:         doi = _extract_doi_from_url(url)
 586:         with httpx.Client(timeout=DEFAULT_HTTP_TIMEOUT) as c:
 587:             if doi:
 588:                 # Exact DOI lookup — most reliable. OpenAlex accepts
 589:                 # both the bare DOI and the URL form; use bare DOI.
 590:                 r = c.get(f"{OPENALEX_ENDPOINT}/doi:{doi}")
 591:                 if r.status_code != 200:
 592:                     # Fall back to title search if DOI not indexed
 593:                     r = c.get(
 594:                         OPENALEX_ENDPOINT,
 595:                         params={
 596:                             "search": (title or url)[:200],
 597:                             "per-page": 1,
 598:                         },
 599:                     )
 600:                     if r.status_code != 200:
 601:                         return {}
 602:                     data = r.json()
 603:                     results = data.get("results", [])
 604:                     if not results:
 605:                         return {}
 606:                     work = results[0]
 607:                 else:
 608:                     work = r.json()  # single-work response from /works/doi
 609:             else:
 610:                 r = c.get(
 611:                     OPENALEX_ENDPOINT,
 612:                     params={
 613:                         "search": (title or url)[:200],
 614:                         "per-page": 1,
 615:                     },
 616:                 )
 617:                 if r.status_code != 200:
 618:                     return {}
 619:                 data = r.json()
 620:                 results = data.get("results", [])
 621:                 if not results:
 622:                     return {}
 623:                 work = results[0]
 624:         primary = work.get("primary_location") or {}
 625:         source = primary.get("source") or {}
 626:         return {
 627:             "openalex_pub_type": work.get("type", "") or "",
 628:             "openalex_source_type": source.get("type", "") or "",
 629:             "is_peer_reviewed": bool(
 630:                 work.get("type") in ("article", "review")
 631:                 and source.get("type") == "journal"
 632:             ),
 633:             "openalex_id": work.get("id", ""),
 634:             # BUG-M-12 (Codex pass 12): preserve OpenAlex's full
 635:             # display_name. Serper snippet titles are often truncated
 636:             # mid-title, losing "systematic review and meta-analysis",
 637:             # "perspective for primary care providers", etc. suffixes
 638:             # that the classifier needs to demote false T1s.
 639:             "openalex_full_title": work.get("display_name", "") or "",
 640:         }
 641:     except Exception as exc:
 642:         logger.debug("[live_retriever] OpenAlex enrich failed for %r: %s", url, exc)
 643:         return {}

1790:             url=cand.url,

1792:             publisher="",

1798:             # BUG-M-17 (Codex pass 2): body-inspection secondary signal.
1799:             body_article_type=body_article_type,
1800:         )

1802:

1804:             url=cand.url,

1806:             domain=domain_,

1808:             tier_confidence=tier_result.confidence,

 succeeded in 1366ms:
   1: """OpenAlex API client for source canonicalization and authority scoring.
   2: 
   3: PATCH-D (SOTA adoption plan). Source: api.openalex.org /works endpoint.
   4: Free public API, no key required. Polite-pool header gives 10× rate
   5: limit by providing an email.
   6: 
   7: Two operations:
   8: 
   9: 1. canonicalize(url, doi, title) -> OpenAlexWork | None
  10:    Returns the canonical OpenAlex work ID, source type, and
  11:    publication type. Used for bibliography dedup across revisions
  12:    (same work at publisher + PMC + institutional-repo collapses to one
  13:    work_id) and for authority-tier gating.
  14: 
  15: 2. OpenAlexWork.authority_tier() -> str
  16:    Maps (type, source.type, is_retracted) to our internal tier:
  17: 
  18:      GOLD:    type in {article, review} AND source.type == journal AND
  19:               NOT is_retracted
  20:      SILVER:  type == preprint OR source.type == repository (preprints,
  21:               institutional repos — legitimate but unreviewed)
  22:      BRONZE:  type in {book-chapter, book, dataset, editorial, letter,
  23:               other} (grey literature, non-primary)
  24:      BLOCKED: is_retracted OR type == erratum
  25: 
  26: Closes the PG_LB_SA_01 defect where Motley Rice law-firm pages, Medium
  27: blog posts, thegutpunch.com, Fella Health telehealth, NHS JS high-school
  28: journal, and a ResearchSquare preprint were all tiered SILVER alongside
  29: SELECT trial data.
  30: """
  31: from __future__ import annotations
  32: 
  33: import logging
  34: import os
  35: import sqlite3
  36: from dataclasses import dataclass
  37: from pathlib import Path
  38: from typing import Optional
  39: 
  40: logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
  41: 
  42: OPENALEX_BASE = "https://api.openalex.org"
  43: POLITE_EMAIL = os.getenv("OPENALEX_EMAIL", "")
  44: CACHE_DB = Path(os.getenv("OPENALEX_CACHE_DB", "cache/openalex.sqlite"))
  45: TIMEOUT = float(os.getenv("OPENALEX_TIMEOUT", "10"))
  46: ENABLED = os.getenv("PG_OPENALEX_ENABLED", "1") == "1"
  47: 
  48: 
  49: @dataclass
  50: class OpenAlexWork:
  51:     """Subset of OpenAlex work fields we need for authority + dedup."""
  52:     work_id: str            # canonical: https://openalex.org/W...
  53:     doi: str                # https://doi.org/10... or ''
  54:     title: str
  55:     type: str               # 'article', 'preprint', 'book-chapter', ...
  56:     source_type: str        # 'journal', 'repository', ...
  57:     source_name: str        # 'Nature Medicine', etc.
  58:     publication_year: int
  59:     is_retracted: bool
  60: 
  61:     def authority_tier(self) -> str:
  62:         if self.is_retracted or self.type == "erratum":
  63:             return "BLOCKED"
  64:         if self.type in {"article", "review"} and self.source_type == "journal":
  65:             return "GOLD"
  66:         if self.type == "preprint" or self.source_type == "repository":
  67:             return "SILVER"
  68:         return "BRONZE"
  69: 
  70:     def authority_tier_t7(
  71:         self,
  72:         *,
  73:         url: str = "",
  74:         source_type_hint: str = "",
  75:         fetched_content_length: int = 0,
  76:         publisher: str = "",
  77:     ) -> str:
  78:         """HONEST-REBUILD Phase 2f — return a T1-T7 tier via the new rules.
  79: 
  80:         Runs the OpenAlex metadata through the Phase 2a tier_classifier
  81:         so regulatory documents (T3), industry marketing (T5), student
  82:         journals (T6), conference abstracts (T7), and other tiers that
  83:         the legacy GOLD/SILVER/BRONZE scheme conflated are separated
  84:         cleanly.
  85: 
  86:         Returns one of: "T1", "T2", "T3", "T4", "T5", "T6", "T7",
  87:         "UNKNOWN". Callers should prefer this over .authority_tier()
  88:         for anything downstream that shows tier distribution to the
  89:         user (Phase 2g corpus-approval gate) or filters synthesis
  90:         (Phase 4 generator).
  91: 
  92:         Retraction check stays here: retracted works are returned as
  93:         "UNKNOWN" to signal they should be excluded from the corpus
  94:         regardless of tier.
  95:         """
  96:         if self.is_retracted or self.type == "erratum":
  97:             return "UNKNOWN"
  98:         from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.tier_classifier import (  # noqa: E402
  99:             ClassificationSignals,
 100:             classify_source_tier,
 101:         )
 102:         sig = ClassificationSignals(
 103:             url=url,
 104:             title=self.title or "",
 105:             publisher=publisher or self.source_name or "",
 106:             fetched_content_length=fetched_content_length,
 107:             openalex_publication_type=self.type or "",
 108:             openalex_source_type=self.source_type or "",
 109:             openalex_is_peer_reviewed=(
 110:                 self.type in {"article", "review"} and self.source_type == "journal"
 111:             ),
 112:             source_type_hint=source_type_hint or "",
 113:         )
 114:         result = classify_source_tier(sig)
 115:         return result.tier.value
 116: 
 117: 
 118: def _cache_init() -> None:
 119:     CACHE_DB.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
 120:     conn = sqlite3.connect(CACHE_DB)
 121:     try:
 122:         conn.execute(
 123:             "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS works ("
 124:             " key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,"
 125:             " work_id TEXT,"
 126:             " doi TEXT,"
 127:             " title TEXT,"
 128:             " type TEXT,"
 129:             " source_type TEXT,"
 130:             " source_name TEXT,"
 131:             " publication_year INTEGER,"
 132:             " is_retracted INTEGER,"
 133:             " fetched_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,"
 134:             " miss INTEGER DEFAULT 0)"
 135:         )
 136:         conn.commit()
 137:     finally:
 138:         conn.close()
 139: 
 140: 
 141: def _cache_get(key: str) -> tuple[Optional[OpenAlexWork], bool]:
 142:     """Return (work, is_miss_record). is_miss_record=True means we've
 143:     previously looked this up and OpenAlex had no match — don't re-query."""
 144:     if not CACHE_DB.exists():
 145:         return None, False
 146:     conn = sqlite3.connect(CACHE_DB)
 147:     try:
 148:         row = conn.execute(
 149:             "SELECT work_id, doi, title, type, source_type, source_name,"
 150:             " publication_year, is_retracted, miss FROM works WHERE key = ?",
 151:             (key,),
 152:         ).fetchone()
 153:     finally:
 154:         conn.close()
 155:     if not row:
 156:         return None, False
 157:     if row[8]:
 158:         return None, True
 159:     return OpenAlexWork(
 160:         work_id=row[0], doi=row[1] or "", title=row[2] or "",
 161:         type=row[3] or "other", source_type=row[4] or "",
 162:         source_name=row[5] or "", publication_year=row[6] or 0,
 163:         is_retracted=bool(row[7]),
 164:     ), False
 165: 
 166: 
 167: def _cache_put(key: str, w: Optional[OpenAlexWork]) -> None:
 168:     conn = sqlite3.connect(CACHE_DB)
 169:     try:
 170:         if w is None:
 171:             conn.execute(
 172:                 "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO works "
 173:                 "(key, work_id, doi, title, type, source_type, source_name,"
 174:                 " publication_year, is_retracted, miss)"
 175:                 " VALUES (?, '', '', '', '', '', '', 0, 0, 1)",
 176:                 (key,),
 177:             )
 178:         else:
 179:             conn.execute(
 180:                 "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO works "
 181:                 "(key, work_id, doi, title, type, source_type, source_name,"
 182:                 " publication_year, is_retracted, miss)"
 183:                 " VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 0)",
 184:                 (key, w.work_id, w.doi, w.title, w.type, w.source_type,
 185:                  w.source_name, w.publication_year, int(w.is_retracted)),
 186:             )
 187:         conn.commit()
 188:     finally:
 189:         conn.close()
 190: 
 191: 
 192: async def _fetch_work(params: dict) -> Optional[dict]:
 193:     import httpx
 194:     headers = {
 195:         "User-Agent": f"POLARIS/1.0 (mailto:{POLITE_EMAIL})"
 196:     } if POLITE_EMAIL else {"User-Agent": "POLARIS/1.0"}
 197:     try:
 198:         async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=TIMEOUT) as c:
 199:             r = await c.get(f"{OPENALEX_BASE}/works", params=params, headers=headers)
 200:             if r.status_code != 200:
 201:                 return None
 202:             data = r.json()
 203:             results = data.get("results", [])
 204:             return results[0] if results else None
 205:     except Exception as exc:
 206:         logger.debug("OpenAlex fetch failed: %s", str(exc)[:200])
 207:         return None
 208: 
 209: 
 210: def _parse_work(data: dict) -> OpenAlexWork:
 211:     primary = data.get("primary_location") or {}
 212:     source = primary.get("source") or {}
 213:     return OpenAlexWork(
 214:         work_id=data.get("id", ""),
 215:         doi=data.get("doi", "") or "",
 216:         title=data.get("title", "") or "",
 217:         type=data.get("type", "other") or "other",
 218:         source_type=source.get("type", "") or "",
 219:         source_name=source.get("display_name", "") or "",
 220:         publication_year=data.get("publication_year", 0) or 0,
 221:         is_retracted=bool(data.get("is_retracted", False)),
 222:     )
 223: 
 224: 
 225: def _fetch_work_sync(params: dict) -> Optional[dict]:
 226:     """Synchronous variant of _fetch_work for callers that aren't async."""
 227:     import httpx
 228:     headers = {
 229:         "User-Agent": f"POLARIS/1.0 (mailto:{POLITE_EMAIL})"
 230:     } if POLITE_EMAIL else {"User-Agent": "POLARIS/1.0"}
 231:     try:
 232:         with httpx.Client(timeout=TIMEOUT) as c:
 233:             r = c.get(f"{OPENALEX_BASE}/works", params=params, headers=headers)
 234:             if r.status_code != 200:
 235:                 return None
 236:             data = r.json()
 237:             results = data.get("results", [])
 238:             return results[0] if results else None
 239:     except Exception as exc:
 240:         logger.debug("OpenAlex sync fetch failed: %s", str(exc)[:200])
